content:
First, thanks for always being open to communicating with your playerbase and taking accountability. That alone means a lot. 

While the unveiling of the 2.5 patch was a rollercoaster, I still can't help but feel disappointed in general. You promised us gradual rollout and noninvasive nature of Absolute Axiom, and then come 2.5, and we saw *everyone* and their mothers getting one in all at the same time, which contradicts your earlier words. Like you've acknowledged in your post, some of them have no business being in Absolute Axiom. 

I'll be honest. One of the things that truly baffles me are the collabs. Collabs are usually (to my knowledge) done to increase revenue and traffic to both of the franchises (?) collaborating. Partnering with niche/obscure or dead games defeats the purpose, personally. I'd rather you guys allocate the funds elsewhere, especially with all the overdue work you've listed in your letter. I know it might not have been solely your decision, but I feel it's better to focus the energy on the game itself. 

I think having a**__ clear roadmap__** for a year would be beneficial for everyone. This would show the players the planned progress and direction of the game while also holding the company accountable of these changes. This way, I think there'll be better picture of what the year's in store for everyone, and you can pace yourself and avoid the issue of trying to fix everything all at once.  Additionally, perhaps consulting the player base *first* before making groundbreaking changes (ie ethereal core) would prevent the raising of pitchforks. 

Nevertheless, despite my feelings, I'm still hoping for the betterment of the game and will be cautiously watching the developments moving forward. I pray this letter doesn't hold empty words.

content:
No game is ever entitled to support. Whether the people behind it are good-natured and passionate or not. Profitability can't be demanded and especially with smaller communities as you have less lee way.

Just as players have to accept that game developers need _some_ way to earn income, developers also need to accept that every player is free to stop supporting their game the instant they don't feel the money put in has a worthy return or they feel unappreciated.

If you want a concrete example, the game Lords of the Fallen came out in 2023 and only some weeks ago broke even. This is entirely because the developers earned the purchases slowly over time with updates and good service. The game isn't very profitable even now, but because of the resulting great reputation and upwards trend in sales long after its initial release the studio behind it secured much bigger funding for their future than a failed launch could reasonably expect.

content:
On the topic of AAs, they definitely should have first been the standard units so you can curve their things out. And making "fixes" to be in their +12 barely hurts due to the nature of how quickly standard units are amassed.

But the limited ones like Wanda being a nothingburger (which good that it's a +12 that doesn't magically fixes the issues with the character)
While others that are already too good like Gmurphy, Mouch.
And then there are others like Doresain who gains immense power  from his AA.

I suggest maybe we work with the community on what AA should be, the easier option would indeed be just buff what the characters do or what the original hopeful plan was to "promote new playstyles"

As for example Doresain gains such a BIG fix with the DMG overkill transfer and the on rouse -20% current enemy hp is such a game changer 🤔 

Personal suggestion.
How about over the course of the version every day or so in discord, THE PLAYERS get to make the AA for the old limited unit. A multiple choice answer for the Devs to pick from US the players. Democracy.

It may be wishful thinking but at that point why not? We are going to be the ones getting them. And along with that buff characters who are dead in the water like Wanda is. It sounds very wishful of me but if players don't want to trust the Devs or think they are incompetent, just let us do it ourselves?

content:
@everyone 

Update: I want to share where we’ve landed on Absolute Axiom and the cases where the design went too far — Doresain being the clearest example. The combat design team and I have just reached an alignment on our approach.

In order for Absolute Axiom to serve its intended purpose — without trapping us in an unnecessary balance spiral — V2.5.1 will move a portion of the overpowered Axiom abilities into the character’s base kit at E0.

Using Doresain as the example: 50% of his damage overflow effect will become part of his base kit.

This change will not alter the final effect of any Axiom already committed to, but it may affect how you plan your Axiom investments going forward. Doresain is likely not the only case — we’ll keep reviewing others.

新进展：关于最终法则与例如杜勒赛因这样设计过头的案例，我们的处理方针刚刚与战斗设计师达成了一致。

为了让最终法则服务原本的预期，不至于在平衡时陷入无序膨胀，我们会在V2.5.1 将一部份过于强大的最终法则部份能力转变为0启时的基础能力。

以杜勒赛因为例，其伤害溢出效果将会有 50% 成为基础能力。

这项改动不会影响任何目前承诺的最终法则最终效果，但可能会影响大家对于最终法则的投资规划。杜勒赛因也可能不是唯一的例子，我们会继续观察。

content:
Ethereal Cores being in paid bundles makes no sense and will harm the game's monetization in the long run, while having no effect on the dreaded gray market resellers who don't spend anyway. Especially with how unreliable this game is with patch releases. Arachne has been getting delayed for close to three months – which is conveniently how long Ethereal Cores last. If this is to happen again with this system in place, especially if communication is as late and poor as it has been up till now, it will discourage regular spending in order to save up for a character we might want. We need a sturdier communication structure, more reliable updates, and a roadmap longer in advance for this change to have even a chance to not harm the game's revenue.

content:
To clarify on one point: Ethereal Cores do serve a different role when they’re inside paid bundles.
What they make possible is offering more pulls per bundle at the same price point — which is especially suited to small, high-value bundles. With Luminous Cores (permanent currency), that kind of pricing is much harder to sustain.
This still fits into the broader framework we’ve been working toward: letting players across different spending levels end up with more pulls, within a financially healthy structure.

content:
Quick clarification on Ethereal Cores:
    ∙    Within a major version window (e.g., the entire V2.5.x cycle), Ethereal Cores function identically to Luminous Cores.
    ∙    Once that version window ends, they automatically convert into Pure Cores.
    ∙    So they sit between the two — Luminous-equivalent during the window, Pure afterwards.

content: <@1487626955791663208> I know this is sort've the direction you're going in and it's hard to retroactively change the AA buffs, but if you're committing to making the AA buffs significant power increases, please consider making them pure stat/damage/power buffs instead of new mechanics. I think it's frustrating, especially for new or free to play players, to have not only the 'power' of a character locked behind AA, but also to have how fun the character is to play locked behind it too (this is also an issue, to a lesser degree, with kits such as Kathigu, who is simply annoying to play at E0). In the long run I worry that new players will get the impression that you can never play the 'proper' version of a character without E12, and this will turn them off the game. Making AA pure power buffs would fix this as it would be clear you can play the character's whole kit with just OE, and the AA is just to make them very strong, while still rewarding whales for their investment.

content:
Yes — buffing and adjusting older Awakeners is part of what V2.5.1 is specifically for. The pattern you’re describing (“old Awakener only becomes playable via pulling the new one”) is something we’ve recognized internally, and it’s not a direction we’re satisfied with either.
I can’t confirm the complete list of characters being adjusted yet, because the plan is still being finalized with the combat design team. What I can say:
∙    Doresain is definitely being addressed — 50% of his AA’s damage overflow effect will move into his 0E base kit.
∙    The full list — including which of the names you mentioned are in scope — will be in the V2.5.1 devlog before it goes live.
Thanks for the specific callouts. Lists like this genuinely help us prioritize.

我讀了你說的”新春會那個人”——那個願意花錢支持同人、會回應每一個玩家的傢伙——我就是那個人。為了支持同人創作——我們騰出了精力製作週邊——並以我個人的名義計劃與前主美溝通——想辦法在尊重美術組成員的前提下——獲得設定集的編輯和發行權。這件事還沒有進展——但我在做。

至於你說的長草期、主線難度、融災、列車——我不辯解。這些問題是真的——它們的根源——是產能不足——是內部儲備耗盡、是需要招人、是需要超前規劃。越是為未來投資——當下產能就越緊。這是一個殘酷的事實——對中文老玩家來說——體會會尤其深。

我不會假裝這是一個我現在就能解決的問題。我只能說——我一直在推進所有該推進的事情。

你說過去那個版本的我贏過你的好感和尊重。謝謝。如果將來有一天——這些推進的事情真的落地了——你偶然回來——看到游戏不一樣了——我會高興。

如果沒有——我也只能理解。

我不是不知道，只是短時間內確實還做不到。

1. Is it possible to collect player feedback (e.g., through surveys) before making major changes, or to open a test server/early access server?

I’ve already spent money on this game—what else can I do? I’m not a top spender who maxes out every character to +12, but I still put money into it from time to time.

I’m even planning to do a Liz cosplay photoshoot in May, and I’ve already spent money on fan creations for your game as well.

It’s not that I don’t love this game—I really do. But right now, I’m genuinely confused about where the game is heading in the future.

Also, regarding the story: could Light  provide a relatively concrete idea of how you plan to address the current production capacity issues?

content:
I’m sorry for the misunderstanding. Let me clarify what the commitment actually meant:

We don’t port existing character adjustments from JP directly into Global — that would change content players already have, which is a line we hold.

However, for new designs on both sides, the two versions do look at each other’s experience. For new Realm designs, we are drawing on similar thinking to what JP has explored — including some of their cooldown-based mechanics — because we’ve found those approaches can release more interesting combinations in a controlled way.

But our version’s execution is more aggressive — in numbers and mechanics — to make sure the step-up from classic Realms is meaningful enough.

content:
回答一：目前没有变化，常规活动不会有空幻。因为每个活动都有区别所以我无法给你提供具体的数字。

回答二：在最终法则最早沟通时就明确了我们绝不会围绕最终法则来进行关卡难度的设计。

回答三：计划按原始赠送银芯数额提供兑换机会，银芯源液1:1。这是考虑到库存情况可能不同所以变为特殊的兑换。直接送则会导致对改动前的玩家不公平。

剧情的部份我们会尽力保持文学性、易懂性的平衡。我们的新文案成员正在逐渐熟悉项目。

繁杂的礼包弹出，的确是个恼人的问题，我们想要在某个时刻进行整理。改善大家的体验。

所有一星皮肤都几乎是活动限定通过任务免费赠送的。之所以可以用源液兑换是因为我们仍然希望提供给错过的人一个弥补收藏的机会，同时保证对之前努力获取免费奖励的人感到不被背叛。这些皮肤都是微小差异没有模型变化，主要是对一些变种和未被使用的历史插画一些使用机会，有玩家跟我们表达想要他们。

谢谢你作为新玩家，愿意信任我们。

content:
<@1487626955791663208> I'm a bit of a pessimist and I'm not as invested in this game as most of the people here, but since you want feedback I'll share.

I quit this game a long time ago because the monetisation got in the way of my enjoyment and recently I came back and quit again because things did not seem to improve.
I can accept that removing excess passes, fomo, etc is unlikely, but what ultimately gets me is that the game has simultaneously _a lot_ of monetisation and it also tries to twist my arm into spending which makes me feel like skipping.

Every menu in Morimens leads to an optional payment; premium tracks, fomo offers when you Awaken, a battle pass and then I could not even enjoy the fun gameplay and interesting story.  Whenever I wanted to build a team, try a new character, get properly invested in your game- I would see monetary barriers. "does this one need exalt3?" "What if they're one of the ones that become useless later?" "Does this wheel need 12 duplicates?" Maybe most characters don't get a big power spike from E1-3, but it still feels like if I pull a character I will be penalized for 'not spending _enough_'. Morimens might technically be free, but it doesn't feel like it.

Now I read today's post and I find that not only are these things still a problem, they are being made worse (AA). You mention that the game is unprofitable and ultimately does not make much money, but you have so much monetisation implemented that it doesn't really add up. I have to assume that there are players like me who are having a service-issue. It feels like no matter where I dare spend any money I will be cheated in some way or miss out on spending somewhere else in the game. I genuinely believe a reason people do not spend much is because the game insists so strongly. Even if we assume the intention behind something like AA/exalt1-3 are pure. Instead of giving people a single, simple, high value option for pay you add more gameplay-leaning incentives.

content:
Some people say that Light deeply values free-to-play players, going out of their way to defend him—as if raising any doubts simply means you “don’t get the bigger picture.” But if we take a step back and think carefully—what if he is actually, little by little, eroding the space that F2P players rely on?

He repeatedly claims that the recent buffs to the “Absolute Axiom” are meant to uplift underpowered permanent characters. On the surface, that sounds reasonable—almost idealistic. But what do the actual results show? Most of the enhancements have clearly been directed toward already powerful, often limited characters that require pulling to obtain. Is this really “balance,” or just a rebranded redistribution of resources?

What makes this even more ironic is that the very characters who have accompanied free-to-play players throughout their journey—the permanent roster that most players can realistically bring to “Absolute Axiom”—are the ones brushed aside in these adjustments. When those who should have been prioritized are overlooked, can we still honestly call this a defense of free-to-play rights? Or is that “concern” merely a slogan meant to sound good?

Taking this a step further, if the level of enhancement for limited characters is already this significant, what might come next? Is it possible that core gameplay mechanics—features that should be accessible to all—could be deliberately segmented and tied to the “Absolute Axiom,” with activation conditions conveniently aligned with those same limited characters? At that point, would achieving a “normal” gameplay experience effectively become contingent on pulling?

If things truly move in that direction, then the issue is no longer just about balance—it’s about whether the entire game environment is quietly shifting. When choices are narrowed and experiences become stratified, can free-to-play players still be said to be “valued”? Or have they already, without realizing it, been pushed to the margins?

content:
Although most debate has already been done I  still want to give a few suggestions.

The additions of Ethereal Cores and Absolute Axiom are not bad per se.
Both of them are expected mechanics to exist in gacha games. But their implementation could have been better done.

**Communication**

Before making changes to core mechanics and aspects of a game there's a need to prepare the playerbase to those changes.
Explaining upcoming changes months prior to the actual changes and the release of such changes across multiple game versions instead of a single version. Likely extending it thorough the whole year.

**Ethereal Cores**

The implementation of those came without notice. Players noticed the change in the teaser images for the upcoming patch. This should not happen.
Code rewards, giveaways and other free rewards that gives Luminous Cores should be changed into Ethereal Cores. While cores given by game modes should remain as Luminous Core or Silver as they require active play to be obtained.

**Absolute Axiom**

Besides the "hasty" implementation there's a huge powergap between absolute axioms across characters. Some are so weak that might as well not exist while other are so strong that might change how the character itself works. 
Releasing it thorough multiple versions would give the team time to make changes to the mechanic as needed.
Dividing the absolute axiom into 2 parts might have been a good idea as there's no effective upgrades to the +8 mark besides increase in stats.

**Overall** I have a very positive view of the game and the dev team outside of a few mishaps.
I do understand that work and passion often intermingle when it comes to game development so I hope that attacks the team receives in case like these do not erase the passion or causes any traumas to you and the other members of the team.

As much negative feedback as you have received during this update. I know that making a live service game is a tight rope to walk.

I know I'm in the minority here, but if the lack of resources, a lack of hands on the project or just burn-out from overwork starts settling. I'd Throw most feedback out the window, and slowly change monetisation strategy, whatever way seems to fit. Even if it's splitting the weekly rewards between the game modes, like the idea you rolled back.

Inconvenience for the player a lot of the time = profit.

A gacha has yet to succeed after changing monetisation, after all everyone loves their free mead. But morimens has already done, and continues to do things that haven't been done.

It's of course, rude and argumentative to say this, but a lot of the feedback given over the last few days by community members on what could give the project more money are genuinely wishful thinking.

Considering the obvious implications of you wanting to do a larger marketing push soon, and not wanting these changes to roll out in a midst of a bunch of new players joining the game... Fair.

I'm genuenly just disappointed by a lot of people here, having this close of a communication with the developers, having the ability to hear their decisions at **certain points in time** and taking it as authority forever. I thought they'd learn but they just keep mentioning old promises.

It's a self loathing self digging hole to come back to whenever a change they don't like comes to the game and that annoys me.

These are just my loose thoughts about all of this. I'm gonna be bombarded with venush saying something like "dev responsibility not to speak"

content:
A lot has been said from Day 1 to present, from both players and the game team. Personally, I'm tired of all of Light's lengthy appeals to player goodwill every time they mess up rather than providing us with clearer communication, an actual roadmap, concrete plans and results that actually match what they promise. In fact, they don't even need to promise us anything more than what they can actually, realistically, deliver considering their resources at the time. I believe a lot of us would rather have delays in releasing new characters instead of the slop we have been getting. A lot of us, f2ps and spenders of all types, have willingly stayed with the game throughout everything.

Instead, we're always fed with the "we're stretched too thin, too broke, too etc etc" reasons, asking for more of our patience and understanding and funds. How many times has it been and how many more times will it be? It's not that we don't understand that they need money. It's that we don't understand why they're making the hasty and questionable choices they have been making.

The collab, for example, if they're so broke then why not redirect the resources used on that to upgrade the quality of the game? If they can allot resources to create a whole new character and write the story, then wouldn't that mean that they actually have the funds to implement the upgrades that they have been telling us for so long?

I remember last time we said we're willing to pay for character and card skins, but they said something along the lines of, unsurprisingly, they don't have the resources to do so. Not even the card skins? They can do a whole lot of the cosmetics they've been doing (underwhelming ones too, btw) but not even card skins?

We also said we'd buy merch, like the artbook or more of the shiny bookmarks (which they didn't even make accessible to global players <:24_Clown:1468259444423921871> ). So many of the more mainstream games profit from merch. Heck, even wall posters would give them something. Is this actually hard to understand? 

If we are to believe the things they have been saying, including the part where they announced that they have secured enough funding to ensure the game continues for at least 2 more years, then they do have the resources to gain funds from us in ways that don't alienate and antagonize players like what has been happening. They do have the resources to solidify the game's quality first, even ensuring that their identity as a lovecraftian deck-building game (as opposed to yet just another weeb gacha) remains the same.

We have come so far, have done so much (unpaid) labor to help out each other and maybe even promote the game to friends. And I believe, even those of us who are increasingly getting dissatisfied and disappointed will still continue staying with the game. I, for one, have done yet another top-up (I have stockholm syndrome <:24_Clown:1468259444423921871> ) despite my misgivings about everything. I hope that Light will always keep in mind that player goodwill is a finite resource, and that he has exhausted a huge amount of it.

content:
# 【制作人公开信】我们绝不会辜负

## I. An Apology

The V2.5 changes — Ethereal Cores, the weekly mission restructure, the simultaneous rollout of Absolute Axioms — were packed into a single patch without being communicated ahead of time through a proper devlog. The confusion and anxiety this caused was real, and it was our doing.

This was not a small misstep. It was a failure of judgment on my part as producer, and the responsibility sits entirely with me.

## II. What Happened

I’m not writing this to make excuses. I want you to see the full picture of where we are — not to ask for sympathy, but so the decisions of the last few months make sense in context.

### The progress after Anniversary 2 — and what it cost us

A few months ago, coming out of our 2nd Anniversary, we made more real progress than we had at any point before. We’re still a long way from where the bigger games are — but that progress was enough to let us keep going, to keep working toward what we set out to do. As the producer, I felt genuinely grateful.

But the problem with progress is: you have to keep going.

We are sitting on a large pile of overdue work:
    ∙    Hiring more developers
    ∙    2.0 content reserve that is almost depleted
    ∙    3.0 content that needs to start being built well in advance
    ∙    Raising the quality bar for our updates
    ∙    More collaborations
    ∙    Starting to explore advertising and marketing
    ∙    Hiring operations staff
    ∙    Financial planning and long-term strategy
    ∙    Merchandise design, production, and after-sales
    ∙    Onboarding, training, and culture-building as the team grows

All of this costs money and energy — and it already exceeds what we can comfortably carry.
